title: Fix super_admin bootstrap and recovery logic in backend

requirements.text: Fix the bootstrap logic in the backend so that when the Admin table is empty and a Principal logs in for the first time, that Principal is assigned the role 'super_admin' instead of 'admin'.

quotes: If Admin table is empty, first logged-in Principal must be assigned role = super_admin.

acceptanceCriteria: When adminUsers table is empty and a Principal calls an authenticated update function, that Principal is inserted with role = 'super_admin'.
Subsequent new Principals are assigned role = 'admin' by default (not 'super_admin').
The role field stores only the string 'super_admin' or 'admin', mutually exclusive.

requirements.text: Add automatic recovery logic inside authenticated update calls that require admin privileges: if super_admin_count == 0, automatically promote the calling Principal's role to 'super_admin' (replace existing 'admin' role). This must not be a separate dedicated function — it must run inline within existing privileged update calls.

quotes: If no super_admin exists in Admin table, automatically promote the current caller to super_admin.
Recovery must be automatic. It should run inside authenticated update calls that require admin privileges. Do NOT create a separate recoverSuperAdmin() function.
This recovery logic should only run when super_admin_count == 0.

acceptanceCriteria: Any authenticated update call that checks admin privileges first checks whether super_admin_count == 0.
If super_admin_count == 0, the caller's existing AdminRecord has its role field overwritten from 'admin' to 'super_admin'.
If the caller does not yet have an AdminRecord and super_admin_count == 0, a new record is created with role = 'super_admin'.
If super_admin_count >= 1, the recovery logic does not run and existing roles are unchanged.
No new public-facing function named recoverSuperAdmin (or similar) is added.
After recovery, the caller can immediately perform super_admin-restricted actions (e.g., create new admins) in the same session.

requirements.text: Update the migration module (backend/migration.mo) to ensure that when migrating legacy admin records, if no record carries role 'super_admin' after migration, the first migrated record is promoted to 'super_admin' rather than leaving all records as 'admin'.

quotes: All existing users have role 'admin'. No super_admin exists.

acceptanceCriteria: After migration runs, at least one AdminRecord has role = 'super_admin'.
If legacy data contains zero super_admin entries, the first record in migration order is promoted to 'super_admin'.
All other migrated records retain role = 'admin'.
Migration does not duplicate or drop any existing AdminRecord entries.

constraints: Do not modify any frontend files.
Do not introduce a separate recoverSuperAdmin() public function.
Role field must remain a single mutually exclusive string: 'super_admin' or 'admin' only.
Recovery logic must only execute when super_admin_count == 0.
Do not add multi-role arrays or additional role flags.
nonGoals: UI or frontend changes of any kind.
Adding new role values beyond 'super_admin' and 'admin'.
Creating a standalone or externally callable recovery endpoint.
Changing any authentication mechanism or Internet Identity integration.
